game_info.title: Red's First Flight
game_info.concept: **Red's First Flight:** Players engage in physics-based puzzle gameplay by launching various Angry Birds from a slingshot to destroy structures and eliminate all pigs on screen. To launch a bird, players drag their finger back on the slingshot to adjust trajectory and power, then release to fire; some birds may have secondary abilities activated by tapping the screen mid-flight. Each of the 390+ levels across 8 episodes presents unique structural challenges, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex pig defenses and a limited number of birds per attempt. Progression involves completing levels to unlock subsequent stages, earning 1 to 3 stars based on efficiency, and discovering hidden Golden Eggs for bonus content. A run ends if all birds are used without eliminating every pig, requiring a restart, while the Mighty Eagle can be deployed once per level to clear particularly challenging layouts.
game_info.description: Launch birds from a slingshot to destroy structures and eliminate all pigs! Drag the bird back to aim and set power, then release to fire. Complete levels by eliminating all pigs with limited birds. Earn up to 3 stars based on your score. Progress through increasingly challenging levels with different structures and defenses.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (Left/Right): Adjust slingshot aim angle
Arrow Keys (Up/Down): Adjust slingshot power
Space: Launch the bird
R: Restart (available on game over screen)
Enter: Start game (from start screen)
Esc: Pause/Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic slingshot mechanics and bird launching. This test validates that birds can be launched with varying power and angles, and that they follow realistic physics trajectories. It ensures the core gameplay loop functions correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically cycles through different power levels (low, medium, high) and angles (steep, medium, shallow) to launch birds. Each bird is launched with a 2-second delay between shots to observe trajectory. The test launches 5 birds with varying parameters.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All 5 birds should launch successfully with visible differences in trajectories based on power and angle settings. Birds should follow parabolic paths and interact realistically with structures. No errors should occur during the launch sequence.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by automatically completing a level. This validates that collision detection works correctly, structures can be destroyed, pigs are eliminated, and the game properly transitions to the win state with star rating calculation.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Automatically aims and launches birds with optimal power and angles to hit structures and eliminate all pigs. Continues launching birds until all pigs are destroyed or birds run out. Uses a calculated trajectory to maximize destruction.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The test should successfully eliminate all pigs within the available birds, trigger the win condition, display the victory screen with star rating (1-3 stars based on score), and allow restart. The game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN phase.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition when running out of birds. This ensures the game correctly handles failure states when players exhaust all available birds without eliminating all pigs, and properly displays the game over screen.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Automatically launches all birds with intentionally poor aim (too high angle and low power) to miss targets. Continues until all birds are used while pigs remain alive.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: After all birds are launched without eliminating all pigs, the game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE phase, display appropriate failure message, show remaining pigs count, and offer restart option via R key.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing structure destruction and physics interactions. This validates that wooden blocks break upon sufficient impact, debris interacts realistically, and chain reactions can occur when structures collapse.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Launches birds directly at the base of structures with high power to cause maximum structural damage. Observes whether blocks break, fall realistically, and create chain reactions that can eliminate pigs indirectly.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Structures should break apart when hit with sufficient force, individual blocks should fall and bounce realistically, and collapsing structures should be able to eliminate pigs through secondary impacts. Score should increase with each block destroyed.
TEST_4.description: Testing structure destruction and physics interactions. This validates that wooden blocks break upon sufficient impact, debris interacts realistically, and chain reactions can occur when structures collapse.

TEST_4.expected: Structures should break apart when hit with sufficient force, individual blocks should fall and bounce realistically, and collapsing structures should be able to eliminate pigs through secondary impacts. Score should increase with each block destroyed.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing scoring system and star rating calculation. This validates that points are awarded correctly for destroying blocks and eliminating pigs, and that star thresholds work properly (1 star for completion, 2 stars for good score, 3 stars for excellent score).
TEST_5.strategy_description: Completes a level with varying efficiency levels - first with minimal destruction (1 star), then with moderate destruction (2 stars), and finally with maximum destruction using fewer birds (3 stars). Tracks score progression throughout.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Score should increase with each destroyed block (+100 points) and eliminated pig (+500 points). Bonus points should be awarded for remaining birds (+1000 each). Star rating should correctly reflect performance: 1 star (all pigs eliminated), 2 stars (score > 2000), 3 stars (score > 4000).

TEST_6.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state management. This ensures the pause system works correctly, physics simulation stops when paused, and the game can resume without issues.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Launches a bird mid-flight, then triggers pause (ESC key). Waits several frames while paused, then resumes. Repeats this cycle multiple times during active gameplay to test state transitions.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: When paused, all physics should freeze, the bird should stop mid-air, and a "PAUSED" overlay should appear. Upon resuming, gameplay should continue exactly where it left off without any jumps or glitches. All game state should be preserved.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing collision detection and pig elimination. This validates that birds colliding with pigs at various velocities cause appropriate damage, pigs are eliminated when health reaches zero, and collision forces are calculated correctly.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Launches birds with varying velocities directly at pigs - low speed (grazing hit), medium speed (solid hit), and high speed (powerful impact). Tests both direct hits and indirect hits through falling debris.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Low-speed collisions should cause minimal damage, medium-speed should cause significant damage, and high-speed collisions should eliminate pigs instantly. Pigs should visually indicate damage and disappear when eliminated. Collision physics should feel responsive and fair.
